principle: Normal context shaping is an agentic capability. The kernel may only intervene when the next LLM call would otherwise physically fail, such as a hard prompt overflow.
normalAgentActions: keep raw context when fidelity matters
compact context deliberately when it improves bounded focus
mix raw evidence and condensed summaries
question a suspicious summary or bad compaction result
request targeted historical research or raw retrieval
emergencyKernelActions: shrink only enough to avoid a guaranteed prompt crash
preserve evidence of the fallback in runtime state
push the agent to request deliberate re-expansion if important history is missing
